758	\subsection{The ambient subquotient induction for the Stacks~00K1 step}
759	\label{subsec:gradedModuleApi}
760	
761	The power-series side of \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational} is fully isolated
762	in the \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) toolkit (\cref{subsec:isRatHilb}). What remains is
763	the \emph{graded-module} side of the Stacks~00K1 induction. Rather than build the
764	kernel and cokernel of multiplication by a degree-one element as graded modules
765	over a smaller quotient ring \(R/(x)\) -- which would force a graded structure on a
766	derived carrier -- we range the induction over \emph{pairs of homogeneous
Let me continue reading the blueprint chapter's Route-2 section, which describes exactly what I need to build.

767	submodules of a single fixed ambient graded module} \(M\). A pair \((N, N')\) with
768	\(N' \le N\) homogeneous submodules of \(M\) stands for the subquotient \(N/N'\); its
769	ambient Hilbert function is the difference \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap M_n) -
770	\dim_\kappa(N' \cap M_n)\). The class of such pairs, equipped with finitely many
771	pairwise-commuting degree-one endomorphisms preserving \(N\) and \(N'\), is
772	\emph{closed} under the kernel and cokernel of one of those endomorphisms, both
773	again presented as ambient subquotient pairs. Consequently no graded quotient ring,
774	graded quotient module, or regrading of a subtype/quotient carrier is ever formed:
775	every object that appears is an ambient family \(n \mapsto N_{\mathrm{aux}} \cap M_n\)
776	of submodules of the fixed \(M\).
777	
778	This subsection records that machinery as a thin layer over existing Mathlib
779	scaffold: Mathlib already provides the notion of a homogeneous submodule
780	(\cref{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}), the homogeneity of a span of
781	homogeneous elements (\cref{lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib}), the degree-shift
782	of multiplication by a homogeneous element
783	(\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}), the rank--nullity identity
784	(\cref{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib}), and the finite-generation
785	transfer along a surjection (\cref{lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}).
786	What is supplied here is the internal direct-sum decomposition of a homogeneous
787	submodule as an ambient family (the split \(G_1\)), the subquotient Hilbert data,
788	the kernel/cokernel closure, the degreewise difference identity, the ambient
789	finiteness transfer, and the induction itself.
790	
791	\emph{Setting for this subsection.} Let \(R = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} R_n\) be a graded
792	ring whose degree-zero part \(R_0 = \kappa\) is a field and which is generated as a
793	\(\kappa\)-algebra in degree one, and let \(M = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} M_n\) be a graded
794	\(\kappa\)-vector space (the fixed ambient module) with finite-dimensional
795	components \(M_n\). All submodules \(N, N', \dots\) below are homogeneous submodules
796	of \(M\), and the relevant endomorphisms are \(\kappa\)-linear maps \(M \to M\)
797	raising degree by one -- typically multiplication by a homogeneous element
798	\(x \in R_1\).
799	
800	\subsubsection*{Mathlib dependency anchors}
801	
802	\begin{lemma}[Homogeneous submodule]
803	  \label{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
804	  \lean{Submodule.IsHomogeneous}
805	  \mathlibok
806	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
807	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.Homogeneous.Submodule}).}
808	  Let \(R = \bigoplus_n R_n\) be a graded ring and \(M = \bigoplus_n M_n\) an
809	  internally graded \(R\)-module. A submodule \(p \subseteq M\) is
810	  \emph{homogeneous} when it is closed under taking homogeneous components:
811	  \(m \in p\) implies each graded piece \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in p\). Equivalently
812	  \(p = \bigoplus_n (p \cap M_n)\) as a set. Mathlib bundles this into a homogeneous
813	  submodule type providing order and extensionality structure; the ambient internal
814	  direct-sum decomposition \(p = \bigoplus_n (M_n \cap p)\) is supplied by the split
815	  \(G_1\) (\cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep},
816	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}).
817	\end{lemma}
818	
819	\begin{lemma}[A span of homogeneous elements is homogeneous]
820	  \label{lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib}
821	  \lean{Ideal.homogeneous_span}
822	  \mathlibok
823	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
824	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.Homogeneous.Ideal}).}
825	  Let \(R = \bigoplus_n R_n\) be a graded ring and \(s \subseteq R\) a set each of
826	  whose elements is homogeneous. Then the ideal \(\mathrm{span}\,s\) is a
827	  homogeneous ideal. In particular, for \(x \in R_1\) the principal ideal \((x)\)
828	  is homogeneous, hence \(xM\) and the auxiliary submodule \(N' + x \cdot N\) are
829	  homogeneous; this homogeneity feeds the kernel/cokernel closure
830	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}).
831	\end{lemma}
832	
833	\begin{lemma}[Rank--nullity]
834	  \label{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib}
835	  \lean{LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker}
836	  \mathlibok
837	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
838	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FiniteDimensional.Lemmas}).}
839	  Let \(\kappa\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space and
840	  \(\varphi : V \to W\) a \(\kappa\)-linear map. Then
841	  \[
842	    \dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}\varphi) + \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)
843	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa V .
844	  \]
845	  This is the rank--nullity identity underlying the degreewise difference identity
846	  \(D_5\) (\cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}).
847	\end{lemma}
848	
849	\begin{lemma}[Finite generation transfers along a surjection]
850	  \label{lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
851	  \lean{Submodule.FG.restrictScalars_of_surjective}
852	  \mathlibok
853	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Finiteness.Basic}).}
854	  Let \(\varphi : R \to A\) be a surjective ring homomorphism and \(M\) an
855	  \(A\)-module that is finitely generated when viewed as an \(R\)-module via
856	  \(\varphi\). Then \(M\) is finitely generated as an \(A\)-module. Applied to the
857	  surjection \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}] \twoheadrightarrow \kappa[t_0, \dots,
858	  t_{r-2}]\) killing the endomorphism \(x = t_{r-1}\) that annihilates the two
859	  subquotients, this transfers their finite generation down one generator
860	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}).
861	\end{lemma}
862	
863	\begin{lemma}[Multiplication by a homogeneous element shifts degree]
864	  \label{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}
865	  \lean{SetLike.IsHomogeneousElem.graded_smul}
866	  \mathlibok
867	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.GradedMulAction}).}
868	  Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a graded family acting on a graded family \(\mathcal{M}\)
869	  via a graded scalar multiplication. If \(a \in R\) is homogeneous of degree
870	  \(i\) -- in particular \(x \in R_1\) -- then for \(m \in M_n\) one has
871	  \(a \cdot m \in M_{i+n}\). This is the degree-shifting property that makes \(xM\)
872	  a homogeneous submodule (with degree-\((n+1)\) part \(x \cdot M_n\)) and that
873	  underlies the ambient identity \(x \cdot N \cap M_{n+1} = x \cdot (N \cap M_n)\)
874	  used in the subquotient closure (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}) and
875	  difference identity (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}).
876	\end{lemma}
877	
878	\subsubsection*{The internal grading of a homogeneous submodule (split $G_1$)}
879	
880	\begin{lemma}\leanok
881	[$G_1$ (independence) -- the family $n \mapsto M_n \cap p$ is independent]
882	  \label{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep}
883	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep}
884	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
885	  Let \(M = \bigoplus_n M_n\) be an internally graded \(\kappa\)-module and let
886	  \(p \subseteq M\) be a homogeneous submodule
887	  (\cref{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}). Then the family
888	  \(n \mapsto M_n \cap p\) of \(\kappa\)-subspaces of \(M\) is independent: for each
889	  \(n\),
890	  \[
891	    (M_n \cap p) \;\cap\; \Bigl(\bigsqcup_{m \ne n} (M_m \cap p)\Bigr) \;=\; 0 .
892	  \]
893	  The whole statement lives in the ambient module \(M\): the subspaces
894	  \(M_n \cap p\) are submodules of \(M\), never of a derived carrier \(\smash{\hat p}\).
895	\end{lemma}
896	
897	\begin{proof}
898	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
899	  The components \(M_n\) of the internal decomposition of \(M\) are independent. The
900	  subfamily \(n \mapsto M_n \cap p\) consists of submodules with \(M_n \cap p \le M_n\),
901	  so independence is inherited: a relation among the \(M_n \cap p\) is in particular a
902	  relation among the \(M_n\), forcing each term to vanish.
903	\end{proof}
904	
905	\begin{lemma}\leanok
906	[$G_1$ (supremum) -- $\bigsqcup_n (M_n \cap p) = p$ for $p$ homogeneous]
907	  \label{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}
908	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_inf_eq}
909	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
910	  Let \(p \subseteq M\) be a homogeneous submodule
911	  (\cref{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}). Then
912	  \[
913	    \bigsqcup_{n} (M_n \cap p) \;=\; p .
914	  \]
915	  Together with the independence of
916	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep}, this is the ambient internal
917	  direct sum \(p = \bigoplus_n (M_n \cap p)\), stated entirely inside \(M\) (no
918	  \(\mathrm{DirectSum.Decomposition}\) on the subtype \(\smash{\hat p}\)).
919	\end{lemma}
920	
921	\begin{proof}
922	  \uses{lem:submodule_isHomogeneous_mathlib}
923	  The inclusion \(\bigsqcup_n (M_n \cap p) \le p\) is clear, since each
924	  \(M_n \cap p \le p\). For the reverse inclusion, take \(m \in p\) and write
925	  \(m = \sum_n \mathrm{proj}_n(m)\) with \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in M_n\) using the
926	  internal decomposition of \(M\). Homogeneity of \(p\) gives
927	  \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in p\), hence \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in M_n \cap p\); thus
928	  \(m\) is a finite sum of elements of the \(M_n \cap p\), i.e.
929	  \(m \in \bigsqcup_n (M_n \cap p)\).
930	\end{proof}
931	
932	\subsubsection*{The ambient subquotient class and its Hilbert function}
933	
934	\begin{definition}[Subquotient Hilbert data]
935	  \label{def:graded_subquotientHilb}
936	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotientHilb}
937	  \uses{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
938	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}
939	  Fix a graded \(\kappa\)-module \(\mathcal{M}\), i.e. \(M\) together with an
940	  internal decomposition \(M = \bigoplus_n \mathcal{M}_n\)
941	  (\(\mathrm{DirectSum.Decomposition}\ \mathcal{M}\)) whose components
942	  \(\mathcal{M}_n\) are finite-dimensional over \(\kappa\). A \emph{subquotient
943	  datum of length \(r\)} consists of:
944	  \begin{itemize}
945	    \item a pair of homogeneous submodules \(N' \le N \subseteq M\); and
946	    \item a family \(t : \{0, \dots, r-1\} \to (M \to_\kappa M)\) of pairwise
947	      commuting \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphisms, each raising degree by one
948	      (\(t_i(\mathcal{M}_n) \subseteq \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\)), each preserving \(N\)
949	      and \(N'\) (\(t_i N \subseteq N\), \(t_i N' \subseteq N'\)); together with
950	    \item a finiteness witness that \(M\) is a finite module over the polynomial
951	      ring \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\) acting through the \(t_i\)
952	      (\(\mathrm{Module.Finite}\)).
953	  \end{itemize}
954	  The pair \((N, N')\) represents the subquotient \(N/N'\). Its \emph{ambient
955	  Hilbert function} is
956	  \[
957	    \mathrm{hilb}(n) \;=\; \Bigl(\dim_\kappa (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
958	      \;-\; \dim_\kappa (N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\Bigr) \;\in\; \mathbb{Q},
959	  \]
960	  the difference of the (finite) \(\kappa\)-dimensions of the ambient intersections,
961	  computed in \(\mathbb{Z}\) and cast to \(\mathbb{Q}\). Since \(N' \le N\) one has
962	  \(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n \le N \cap \mathcal{M}_n\), so
963	  \(\mathrm{hilb}(n) = \dim_\kappa\bigl((N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\bigr)\)
964	  is the dimension of the degree-\(n\) piece of \(N/N'\). The split \(G_1\)
965	  (\cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep},
966	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}) guarantees that these ambient
967	  intersections assemble the homogeneous submodules \(N, N'\), so the data depends
968	  only on the ambient families \(n \mapsto N \cap \mathcal{M}_n\),
969	  \(n \mapsto N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n\).
970	\end{definition}
971	
972	\subsubsection*{Closure under kernel and cokernel}
973	
974	\begin{lemma}[Kernel/cokernel closure of the subquotient class]
975	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}
976	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotient_ker_coker}
977	  \uses{def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
978	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
979	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}
980	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}) with
981	  endomorphisms \(t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}\), and set \(x = t_{r-1}\). Form the two new
982	  pairs of homogeneous submodules of \(M\)
983	  \[
984	    K \;=\; \bigl(\,N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\ N'\,\bigr),
985	    \qquad
986	    C \;=\; \bigl(\,N,\ N' + x \cdot N\,\bigr),
987	  \]
988	  where \(x^{-1}(N') = \{\,m \in M : x \cdot m \in N'\,\}\) is the preimage and
989	  \(x \cdot N\) is the image of \(N\) under \(x\). Then:
990	  \begin{enumerate}
991	    \item \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) and \(N' + x \cdot N\) are homogeneous submodules of
992	      \(M\), with \(N' \le N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) and \(N' + x \cdot N \le N\), so both
993	      \(K\) and \(C\) are subquotient pairs;
994	    \item the remaining endomorphisms \(t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}\) pairwise commute,
995	      raise degree by one, and preserve both members of \(K\) and of \(C\); and
996	    \item \(x\) annihilates both subquotients: \(x \cdot (N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \subseteq N'\)
997	      and \(x \cdot N \subseteq N' + x \cdot N\).
998	  \end{enumerate}
999	  Consequently \(K\) and \(C\) are again subquotient data, now of length \(r-1\)
1000	  (the endomorphisms \(t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}\)), and \(K = \ker x\) and \(C =
1001	  \operatorname{coker} x\) on the subquotient \(N/N'\): \(K\) represents
1002	  \(\ker\bigl(x : N/N' \to N/N'\bigr)\) and \(C\) represents
1003	  \(\operatorname{coker}\bigl(x : N/N' \to N/N'\bigr)\). No quotient or subtype
1004	  carrier is constructed; \(K\) and \(C\) are ambient pairs of homogeneous
1005	  submodules of the fixed \(M\).
1006	\end{lemma}
1007	
1008	\begin{proof}
1009	  \uses{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
1010	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
1011	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}
1012	  \emph{Homogeneity.} The preimage \(x^{-1}(N')\) is homogeneous: if
1013	  \(m = \sum_n \mathrm{proj}_n(m)\) with \(x \cdot m \in N'\), then since \(x\) raises
1014	  degree by one (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}) the components
1015	  \(x \cdot \mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\) lie in distinct degrees, and
1016	  homogeneity of \(N'\) (split \(G_1\),
1017	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}) forces each
1018	  \(x \cdot \mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in N'\), i.e.\ \(\mathrm{proj}_n(m) \in x^{-1}(N')\).
1019	  Thus \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) is an intersection of homogeneous submodules, hence
1020	  homogeneous. The image \(x \cdot N\) is homogeneous with degree-\((n+1)\) piece
1021	  \(x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\) by
1022	  \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}, and a sum of homogeneous
1023	  submodules is homogeneous (\cref{lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib} applied at the
1024	  module level), so \(N' + x \cdot N\) is homogeneous. The inclusions
1025	  \(N' \le N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) (as \(x \cdot N' \subseteq N'\)) and
1026	  \(N' + x \cdot N \le N\) (as \(N' \le N\) and \(x \cdot N \subseteq N\)) are
1027	  immediate.
1028	
1029	  \emph{Preservation.} Each \(t_i\) with \(i \le r-2\) commutes with \(x\) and
1030	  preserves \(N, N'\); hence it preserves \(x^{-1}(N')\) (if \(x t_i m = t_i x m \in
1031	  N'\) whenever \(x m \in N'\)), so it preserves \(N \cap x^{-1}(N')\), and it
1032	  preserves \(x \cdot N\) (as \(t_i x N = x t_i N \subseteq x N\)), so it preserves
1033	  \(N' + x \cdot N\). Degree-raising and commutativity are inherited.
1034	
1035	  \emph{Annihilation by \(x\).} For \(m \in N \cap x^{-1}(N')\) one has
1036	  \(x \cdot m \in N'\) by definition, so \(x\) carries the first member of \(K\) into
1037	  its second; and \(x \cdot N \subseteq N' + x \cdot N\) trivially. Thus \(x\) acts as
1038	  zero on \(N/N'\) restricted to \(K\) and on the cokernel quotient \(C\).
1039	
1040	  \emph{Identification with \(\ker x\) and \(\operatorname{coker} x\).} On the
1041	  subquotient \(N/N'\), the class of \(m \in N\) is killed by \(x\) iff
1042	  \(x \cdot m \in N'\), i.e.\ iff \(m \in N \cap x^{-1}(N')\); so \(\ker(x : N/N' \to
1043	  N/N')\) is exactly \((N \cap x^{-1}(N'))/N'\), represented by \(K\). The image of
1044	  \(x\) on \(N/N'\) is \((N' + x \cdot N)/N'\), so the cokernel
1045	  \((N/N')/\operatorname{im} x = N/(N' + x \cdot N)\) is represented by \(C\).
1046	\end{proof}
1047	
1048	\subsubsection*{The subquotient degreewise difference identity ($D_6$)}
1049	
1050	\begin{lemma}[$D_6$ -- subquotient degreewise difference]
1051	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}
1052	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotient_degreewise_diff}
1053	  \uses{def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1054	    lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff, lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
1055	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq,
1056	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}
1057	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
1058	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
1059	  % 00K1), proof (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13939-L13947).
1060	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1 (degreewise short exact sequence).}
1061	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}) with
1062	  Hilbert function \(h_M\), and let \(x = t_{r-1}\). Let \(h_K\) and \(h_C\) be the
1063	  Hilbert functions of the kernel and cokernel subquotients
1064	  \(K = (N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N')\) and \(C = (N,\, N' + x \cdot N)\) of
1065	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}. Then for every \(n\)
1066	  \[
1067	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) \;=\; h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
1068	  \]
1069	  Writing \(Q_n = (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\) for the
1070	  degree-\(n\) piece of \(N/N'\) (so \(\dim_\kappa Q_n = h_M(n)\)), multiplication by
1071	  \(x\) is a \(\kappa\)-linear map \(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1}\) with
1072	  \(\ker(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1})\) the degree-\(n\) piece of \(K\) and
1073	  \(Q_{n+1}/\operatorname{im} x\) the degree-\((n+1)\) piece of \(C\); the identity is
1074	  then the degreewise rank--nullity difference \(D_5\)
1075	  (\cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}). Specialising to \((N, N') = (\top,
1076	  \bot)\) recovers the classical \(0 \to \mathcal{M}_n \xrightarrow{x}
1077	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1} \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1}/x\mathcal{M}_n \to 0\).
1078	\end{lemma}
1079	
1080	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, inductive step (read
1081	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13939-L13947).
1082	% "Let $\overline{M} = M/xM$. Note that the map $x : M \to M$
1083	%  is {\it not} a map of graded $S$-modules, since it does
1084	%  not map $M_d$ into $M_d$. Namely, for each $d$ we have the
1085	%  following short exact sequence
1086	%  $$
1087	%  0 \to M_d \xrightarrow{x} M_{d + 1} \to \overline{M}_{d + 1} \to 0
1088	%  $$
1089	%  This proves that $[M_{d + 1}] - [M_d] = [\overline{M}_{d + 1}]$.
1090	%  Hence we win by Lemma \ref{lemma-numerical-polynomial}."
1091	\begin{proof}
1092	  \uses{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker, lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff,
1093	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq,
1094	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}
1095	  Since \(N' \le N\) are homogeneous, the split \(G_1\)
1096	  (\cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq}) identifies the degree-\(n\) piece
1097	  of \(N/N'\) with \(Q_n = (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\), a
1098	  finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space of dimension \(h_M(n)\). Because \(x\)
1099	  raises degree by one and preserves \(N\) and \(N'\)
1100	  (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}), it induces a \(\kappa\)-linear
1101	  map \(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1}\).
1102	
1103	  \emph{Kernel.} A class \([m] \in Q_n\) (with \(m \in N \cap \mathcal{M}_n\)) lies in
1104	  \(\ker(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1})\) iff \(x \cdot m \in N' \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\), i.e.\
1105	  iff \(m \in (N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \cap \mathcal{M}_n\). Hence \(\ker(x : Q_n \to
1106	  Q_{n+1}) = \bigl((N \cap x^{-1}(N')) \cap \mathcal{M}_n\bigr)/(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\)
1107	  is exactly the degree-\(n\) piece of \(K\), of dimension \(h_K(n)\)
1108	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}).
1109	
1110	  \emph{Cokernel.} The image of \(x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1}\) is
1111	  \(\bigl(x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n) + (N' \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\bigr)/(N' \cap
1112	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\). Using the ambient identity \((N' + x \cdot N) \cap
1113	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1} = (N' \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1}) + x \cdot (N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\)
1114	  -- which holds because \(x \cdot N\) has degree-\((n+1)\) piece \(x \cdot (N \cap
1115	  \mathcal{M}_n)\) (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}) and \(N'\) is
1116	  homogeneous -- the cokernel \(Q_{n+1}/\operatorname{im} x = (N \cap
1117	  \mathcal{M}_{n+1})/\bigl((N' + x \cdot N) \cap \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\bigr)\) is the
1118	  degree-\((n+1)\) piece of \(C\), of dimension \(h_C(n+1)\).
1119	
1120	  \emph{Difference.} Apply the degreewise rank--nullity difference \(D_5\)
1121	  (\cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}) to \(\varphi = (x : Q_n \to Q_{n+1})\),
1122	  with \(V = Q_n\), \(W = Q_{n+1}\):
1123	  \[
1124	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n)
1125	      = \dim_\kappa Q_{n+1} - \dim_\kappa Q_n
1126	      = \dim_\kappa(Q_{n+1}/\operatorname{im}\varphi) - \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)
1127	      = h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
1128	  \]
1129	  This is the \(\mathrm{hdiff}\) hypothesis consumed by \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}.
1130	\end{proof}
1131	
1132	\subsubsection*{Ambient finiteness transfer}
1133	
1134	\begin{lemma}[Finiteness transfer for the subquotients]
1135	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}
1136	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotient_finite_transfer}
1137	  \uses{def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1138	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
1139	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum of length \(r \ge 1\)
1140	  (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}), so that \(M\) is finite over
1141	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\), and let \(x = t_{r-1}\). Then the two
1142	  subquotients
1143	  \[
1144	    K = (N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N'), \qquad C = (N,\, N' + x \cdot N)
1145	  \]
1146	  of \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker} are finite modules over
1147	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]\); that is, they are subquotient data of length
1148	  \(r-1\) with a valid \(\mathrm{Module.Finite}\) witness. This is the finiteness
1149	  needed for the inductive hypothesis of \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}.
1150	\end{lemma}
1151	
1152	\begin{proof}
1153	  \uses{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1154	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
1155	  Since \(M\) is finite over the Noetherian ring
1156	  \(A = \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}]\), \(M\) is a Noetherian \(A\)-module, so the
1157	  submodule \(N \cap x^{-1}(N') \subseteq M\) is finite over \(A\) and the quotient
1158	  \(N/(N' + x \cdot N)\) of the submodule \(N\) is finite over \(A\); likewise
1159	  \((N \cap x^{-1}(N'))/N'\) is finite over \(A\). By
1160	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker} the element \(x = t_{r-1}\) annihilates
1161	  both subquotients, so the \(A\)-action on each factors through the surjection
1162	  \[
1163	    A = \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-1}] \twoheadrightarrow \kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]
1164	  \]
1165	  sending \(t_{r-1} \mapsto 0\). Applying the finite-generation transfer along a
1166	  surjection (\cref{lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}) to this
1167	  surjection turns finiteness over \(A\) into finiteness over
1168	  \(\kappa[t_0, \dots, t_{r-2}]\). Everything is about finite generation of ambient
1169	  submodules and their quotients; no derived-carrier grading is used.
1170	\end{proof}
1171	
1172	\subsubsection*{The degreewise difference identity}
1173	
1174	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1175	[$D_5$ -- degreewise rank--nullity difference]
1176	  \label{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}
1177	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.degreewise_finrank_diff}
1178	  \uses{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib,
1179	    lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
1180	  Let \(\varphi : V \to W\) be a \(\kappa\)-linear map between finite-dimensional
1181	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. Then
1182	  \[
1183	    \dim_\kappa W - \dim_\kappa V
1184	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi) - \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi).
1185	  \]
1186	  Applied degreewise with \(\varphi = (x : \mathcal{M}_n \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\),
1187	  \(V = \mathcal{M}_n\), \(W = \mathcal{M}_{n+1}\) -- so that \(\ker\varphi\) is the
1188	  degree-\(n\) piece \(K_n\) of the kernel subquotient and
1189	  \(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi = \mathcal{M}_{n+1}/x \mathcal{M}_n\) the
1190	  degree-\((n+1)\) piece \(C_{n+1}\) of the cokernel subquotient
1191	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}, via the split \(G_1\)
1192	  \cref{lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq} and the degree shift
1193	  \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}) -- this gives, for every \(n\),
1194	  \[
1195	    \dim_\kappa \mathcal{M}_{n+1} - \dim_\kappa \mathcal{M}_n
1196	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa C_{n+1} - \dim_\kappa K_n,
1197	  \]
1198	  which is the difference identity \(\mathrm{hdiff}\) consumed by
1199	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}. No graded structure is used in proving the
1200	  underlying vector-space identity -- it is pure rank--nullity.
1201	\end{lemma}
1202	
1203	\begin{proof}
1204	  \uses{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib,
1205	    lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
1206	  By rank--nullity (\cref{lem:finrank_range_add_finrank_ker_mathlib}),
1207	  \(\dim_\kappa V = \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi) + \dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}
1208	  \varphi)\), so \(\dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}\varphi) = \dim_\kappa V -
1209	  \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)\). By additivity on the short exact sequence
1210	  \(0 \to \operatorname{range}\varphi \to W \to W/\operatorname{range}\varphi \to 0\)
1211	  (\cref{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}),
1212	  \(\dim_\kappa(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi) = \dim_\kappa W -
1213	  \dim_\kappa(\operatorname{range}\varphi) = \dim_\kappa W - \dim_\kappa V +
1214	  \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)\). Rearranging gives
1215	  \(\dim_\kappa W - \dim_\kappa V = \dim_\kappa(W/\operatorname{range}\varphi) -
1216	  \dim_\kappa(\ker\varphi)\). The degreewise application identifies
1217	  \(\ker(x : \mathcal{M}_n \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\) and
1218	  \(\mathcal{M}_{n+1}/\operatorname{range}(x : \mathcal{M}_n \to \mathcal{M}_{n+1})\)
1219	  with the degree-\(n\) piece of the kernel subquotient and the degree-\((n+1)\)
1220	  piece of the cokernel subquotient (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}),
1221	  the latter because the degree-\((n+1)\) graded piece of the homogeneous submodule
1222	  \(x\mathcal{M}\) is exactly \(x \mathcal{M}_n\)
1223	  (\cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}).
1224	\end{proof}
1225	
1226	\subsubsection*{The ambient subquotient induction}
1227	
1228	\begin{lemma}[Rationality of the ambient subquotient Hilbert function]
1229	  \label{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}
1230	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule.subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational}
1231	  \uses{def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1232	    lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff,
1233	    lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero,
1234	    lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq, lem:ratHilb_bump}
1235	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
1236	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
1237	  % 00K1), proof (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13908-L13914).
1238	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1 (induction on the number of
1239	  degree-one generators).}
1240	  Let \((N, N')\) be a subquotient datum of length \(r\)
1241	  (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}) with ambient Hilbert function
1242	  \(\mathrm{hilb}\). Then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(\mathrm{hilb}, r)\)
1243	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}): the difference \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
1244	  - \dim_\kappa(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n)\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), the coefficient
1245	  sequence of a rational series \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-r}\) with \(p \in
1246	  \mathbb{Q}[X]\). The induction is on the length \(r\), and every object that
1247	  appears is an ambient pair of homogeneous submodules of the fixed graded module
1248	  \(M\).
1249	\end{lemma}
1250	
1251	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, induction set-up (read
1252	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13908-L13914).
1253	% "We prove this by induction on the minimal number of
1254	%  generators of $S_1$. If this number is $0$, then
1255	%  $M_n = 0$ for all $n \gg 0$ and the result holds.
1256	%  To prove the induction step, let $x\in S_1$
1257	%  be one of a minimal set of generators, such that
1258	%  the induction hypothesis applies to the
1259	%  graded ring $S/(x)$."
1260	\begin{proof}
1261	  \uses{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
1262	    lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff,
1263	    lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero,
1264	    lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq, lem:ratHilb_bump}
1265	  We induct on the length \(r\).
1266	
1267	  \emph{Base case \(r = 0\).} With no endomorphisms, the finiteness witness says
1268	  \(M\) is a finite module over \(\kappa[\,] = \kappa\), so \(M\) is a
1269	  finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space. Hence \(N \cap \mathcal{M}_n = 0\) and
1270	  \(N' \cap \mathcal{M}_n = 0\) for all \(n \gg 0\), so \(\mathrm{hilb}(n) = 0\) for
1271	  \(n \gg 0\). By \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero} an eventually-zero function is
1272	  rational of order \(0\), giving \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(\mathrm{hilb}, 0)\).
1273	
1274	  \emph{Inductive step \(r \ge 1\).} Set \(x = t_{r-1}\). By the kernel/cokernel
1275	  closure (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}) the kernel subquotient
1276	  \(K = (N \cap x^{-1}(N'),\, N')\) and the cokernel subquotient
1277	  \(C = (N,\, N' + x \cdot N)\) are subquotient data of length \(r-1\), with valid
1278	  finiteness witnesses supplied by the finiteness transfer
1279	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}). The induction hypothesis applied
1280	  to \(K\) and \(C\) gives \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_K, r-1)\) and
1281	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_C, r-1)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}). By the degreewise
1282	  difference identity (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}),
1283	  \[
1284	    \mathrm{hilb}(n+1) - \mathrm{hilb}(n) \;=\; h_C(n+1) - h_K(n)
1285	    \qquad \text{for all } n .
1286	  \]
1287	  Feeding \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_C, r-1)\), \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_K, r-1)\) and this
1288	  difference identity into the inductive-step engine
1289	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}) yields \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(\mathrm{hilb}, r)\). (The
1290	  common order \(r-1\) for \(h_C\) and \(h_K\) is arranged, if necessary, by raising
1291	  the lower one with \cref{lem:ratHilb_bump}.) This completes the induction.
1292	\end{proof}
1293	
1294	\section{Support and freeness predicates}
1295	\label{sec:quot_predicates}
1296	
1297	The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
1298	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
1299	geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
1300	proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
1301	free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.
1302	
1303	\begin{definition}
1304	
1305	\leanok
1306	[Annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules]
1307	  \label{def:modules_annihilator}
1308	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator}
1309	  \uses{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1310	  % NOTE: The definition itself is formalized and axiom-clean; the inclusion direction
1311	  % \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U) \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\)
1312	  % (\cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}) is closed. The reverse inclusion, which identifies
1313	  % the annihilator sheaf with the module-annihilator on affine opens, remains bridge-gated on
1314	  % \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
1315	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
1316	  % Polynomials" (read from
1317	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
1318	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1319	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
1320	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
1321	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
1322	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
1323	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
1324	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
1325	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
1326	  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
1327	  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
1328	  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
1329	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
1330	  \(U \subseteq X\),
1331	  \[
1332	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
1333	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
1334	    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
1335	  \]
1336	  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
1337	  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
1338	  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).
1339	
1340	  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
1341	  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
1342	  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
1343	  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
1344	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
1345	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
1346	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
1347	  hypotheses enter. The bridge
1348	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
1349	  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
1350	  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
1351	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
1352	  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
1353	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
1354	  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
1355	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
1356	  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
1357	  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
1358	  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
1359	  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
1360	  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
1361	  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.
1362	
1363	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not carry an annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
1364	\end{definition}
1365	
1366	\begin{lemma}\leanok

1	# Analogy: graded-module quotient / kernel / regrading API for Stacks 00K1

12	## Question
13	For the graded-module side of the Stacks 00K1 inductive step (graded Hilbert–Serre
14	rationality, Lean target `AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`):
15	(1) does Mathlib already supply the graded quotient `M/xM`, graded kernel
16	`K = ker(x⋆)`, regrading over `R/(x)`, and graded `Module.Finite` transfer; (2) what
17	is the Mathlib-aligned shape for these defs; (3) is there a route that avoids the
18	graded quotient/kernel entirely and works only with the degreewise `finrank`
19	identity?
20	
21	## Project artifact(s)
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:425-612` — the `IsRatHilb` power-series
23	  toolkit (`IsRatHilb`, `.ofEventuallyZero`, `.bump`, `.sub`, `.shiftRight`,
24	  `.antidiff`, `.ofDiffEq`). DONE and axiom-clean. `ofDiffEq` is the inductive-step
25	  engine: it consumes `hC' : IsRatHilb hC d`, `hK' : IsRatHilb hK d`, and a
26	  difference identity, and produces `IsRatHilb hM (d+1)`.
27	- `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex:346-474` — `lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational`
28	  statement + proof. The proof inducts on the number `r` of degree-one κ-algebra
29	  generators; the inductive step applies the IH to `K` and `C = M/xM` **as f.g.
30	  graded modules over `R/(x)`** (an `(r−1)`-generated ring).
31	
32	## Mathlib state (verified at the pinned commit)
33	
34	### Present (CORRECTS iter-012 on one point)
35	A homogeneous-submodule scaffold now exists; iter-012's "NO graded submodule API" is
36	out of date. But it is a thin scaffold — SetLike/PartialOrder/ext only, **no induced
37	grading**.
38	- `Submodule.IsHomogeneous ℳ p` — `RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/Homogeneous/Submodule.lean:52`.
39	- `HomogeneousSubmodule 𝒜 ℳ` (bundled) — `…/Homogeneous/Submodule.lean:64`. File ends
40	  ~line 130: only `SetLike`, `PartialOrder`, `ext`, `mem_toSubmodule_iff`.
41	- `HomogeneousIdeal 𝒜 := HomogeneousSubmodule 𝒜 𝒜`, `Ideal.IsHomogeneous` —
42	  `…/Homogeneous/Ideal.lean:64,71`. `HomogeneousIdeal.map/comap` — `…/Homogeneous/Maps.lean:34,52`.
43	- `HomogeneousSubsemiring 𝒜` (bundled, scaffold-only) — `…/Homogeneous/Subsemiring.lean:38`.
44	- `Ideal.homogeneous_span` (so `(x)` for `x ∈ R₁` is a `HomogeneousIdeal`) —
45	  `…/Homogeneous/Ideal.lean:159`.
46	- `SetLike.IsHomogeneousElem` + `SetLike.IsHomogeneousElem.graded_smul`
47	  (mul-by-homogeneous sends homogeneous to homogeneous) — `Algebra/GradedMulAction.lean:136`.
48	- Graded-module primitives: `DirectSum.Decomposition` (`Algebra/DirectSum/Decomposition.lean`),
49	  `SetLike.GradedSMul`, the `GradedModule`/`Gmodule` toolkit (`Algebra/Module/GradedModule.lean`),
50	  `GradedRing`.
51	- Linear-algebra inputs for the degreewise identity: `LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`
52	  (rank–nullity), `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` (`LinearAlgebra/Dimension/RankNullity.lean:208`).
53	- Finiteness glue: `Submodule.FG.restrictScalars_of_surjective` (`RingTheory/Finiteness/Ideal.lean:48`).
54	- Converse Hilbert extraction only: `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly`
55	  (`RingTheory/Polynomial/HilbertPoly.lean`).
56	
57	### Absent (CONFIRMS iter-012)
58	- **No `DirectSum.Decomposition` on a `HomogeneousSubmodule`** — the induced grading on
59	  the submodule itself. (grep over `Mathlib/` for `IsHomogeneous`/`⊓ ℳ` → Decomposition: none.)
60	- **No `GradedRing`/`DirectSum.Decomposition` on a quotient ring `A ⧸ I`** for homogeneous
61	  `I`. The entire `RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/` directory contains **zero** occurrences of `⧸`.
62	- **No `DirectSum.Decomposition` on a quotient module `M ⧸ p`** for homogeneous `p`.
63	- **No graded kernel** of a degree-shifting (mul-by-homogeneous) endomorphism.
64	- **No regrading** along a graded surjection (`R ↠ R/(x)`).
65	- **No graded `Module.Finite` transfer** through any of the above.
66	- **No Hilbert series / Hilbert-Serre for graded modules** (only the converse extraction).
67	
68	## Decisions identified
69	
70	### Decision D1: graded quotient `C = M/xM` carrying a `DirectSum.Decomposition`
71	- **Mathlib idiom**: none ships, but the *shape* to mirror is `HomogeneousIdeal` /
72	  `GradedRing`: a grading is an **unbundled `DirectSum.Decomposition` instance** on a
73	  family `ℕ → Submodule …`. `xM` is a homogeneous submodule (image of mul-by-`x`,
74	  `x` homogeneous via `SetLike.IsHomogeneousElem.graded_smul`); the quotient's
75	  components are `(ℳ n).map (Submodule.mkQ (xM))`.
76	- **Gap**: divergent-and-missing → must build. General lemma G2 below.
77	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
78	
79	### Decision D2: graded kernel `K = ker(x⋆ : M → M(1))`
80	- `K` **is** a `HomogeneousSubmodule 𝒜 ℳ` — reuse Mathlib's scaffold. `K = LinearMap.ker`
81	  of mul-by-`x`; an element lies in `K` iff each homogeneous component does (`x·mₙ ∈ ℳ_{n+1}`
82	  are independent), so `Submodule.IsHomogeneous` holds. The induced grading
83	  (`fun n => Kₙ := K ⊓ ℳ n` as a submodule of `M`) needs a `DirectSum.Decomposition`,
84	  which the scaffold does **not** provide. General lemma G1 below.
85	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
86	
87	### Decision D3: regrading — `C`, `K` as graded modules over `R/(x)`
88	- `(x)` is a `HomogeneousIdeal` (`Ideal.homogeneous_span`). Two options:
89	  - **D3a (quotient ring, mirrors Stacks + blueprint)**: build `GradedRing` on `R ⧸ (x)`
90	    (components `(𝒜 n).map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (x))`), then `SetLike.GradedSMul` for the
91	    `R/(x)`-action on `C`, `K`. Needs the missing graded-quotient-ring construction (G3).
92	  - **D3b (graded subring, avoids the quotient ring)**: restrict scalars to the graded
93	    κ-subalgebra `R' = κ[x₁,…,x_{r−1}] ⊆ R`. Since `x_r` annihilates `C`, `K`, they are
94	    `R'`-modules with the **same** grading. Avoids quotient-ring grading but needs a
95	    `GradedRing` instance on a homogeneous subsemiring/subalgebra (also missing) +
96	    `RestrictScalars` graded compatibility.
97	- Both need a "grading on a derived ring" construction Mathlib lacks; D3a is closer to
98	  the existing `HomogeneousIdeal` API and the blueprint. **Recommend D3a.**
99	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
100	
101	### Decision D4: `Module.Finite` transfer (graded, over `R/(x)`)
102	- `C`, `K` are f.g. over `R` (kernel/quotient of f.g. over Noetherian `R`) and
103	  annihilated by `(x)`; `Submodule.FG.restrictScalars_of_surjective` (along `R ↠ R/(x)`)
104	  is the engine. Thin glue, no new theory — but it is glue that must be written.
105	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (low effort).
106	
107	### Decision D5: degreewise SES / `finrank` identity (the Q3 "avoidance" item)
108	- `dim Mₙ₊₁ − dim Mₙ = dim Cₙ₊₁ − dim Kₙ` is **pure linear algebra** on the κ-linear map
109	  `xₙ : Mₙ → Mₙ₊₁`: rank–nullity (`LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`) gives
110	  `dim Kₙ = dim Mₙ − rk xₙ` and `dim(Mₙ₊₁/im xₙ) = dim Mₙ₊₁ − rk xₙ`; subtract. **No graded
111	  object is needed for this identity.** This is exactly the `hdiff` hypothesis of
112	  `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` — and it was already the trivial input.
113	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (build with pure linear algebra; needs no graded API).
114	
115	### Q3 — does the avoidance route collapse the sub-build? NO.
116	The degreewise identity (D5) genuinely avoids graded objects, but it is not the
117	obstruction. `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` *also* demands `hC' : IsRatHilb hC d` and
118	`hK' : IsRatHilb hK d`. The **only** source of those is the inductive hypothesis applied
119	to `C` and `K`, and the IH quantifies over **f.g. graded modules over an `(r−1)`-generated
120	ring**. To instantiate it, `C` and `K` must be presented as such objects — i.e. with
121	`DirectSum.Decomposition` (D1, D2), an `R/(x)`- or `R'`-module structure (D3), and
122	`Module.Finite` (D4). No reformulation feeds the IH "abstract vector-space families":
123	the recursion's payload *is* the graded structure. Free-resolution and graded-dévissage
124	reformulations exist but are strictly **harder** (Hilbert syzygy / graded primes — both
125	also absent from Mathlib). So Q3 removes D5 from the graded build (good, do it) but leaves
126	D1–D4 required.
127	
128	## Recommendation
129	
130	Build a small **graded-module-grading library** (genuine Mathlib gap-fill, all as
131	unbundled `DirectSum.Decomposition`/`SetLike.GradedSMul` instances mirroring
132	`HomogeneousIdeal`/`GradedRing`), then assemble Stacks 00K1 on top. Concrete plan:
133	
134	- **G1 — grading on a homogeneous submodule.** For `p : Submodule A M` with
135	  `p.IsHomogeneous ℳ`, produce `DirectSum.Decomposition (fun n => (p ⊓ ℳ n) as Submodule)`
136	  + `SetLike.GradedSMul 𝒜 (…)`. Gives `K` (D2) and `xM` graded. Generalises cleanly →
137	  PR-worthy. Mirror file: `RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/Homogeneous/Submodule.lean`.
138	- **G2 — grading on the quotient by a homogeneous submodule.** For `p.IsHomogeneous ℳ`,
139	  `DirectSum.Decomposition` on `M ⧸ p` with components `(ℳ n).map (p.mkQ)`. Gives `C` (D1).
140	  Same file. Proof spine: `M = ⨁ ℳ n` internal, `p = ⨁ (p ⊓ ℳ n)` (from G1), so
141	  `M/p = ⨁ ℳ n/(p ⊓ ℳ n)`.
142	- **G3 — grading on `R ⧸ I` for homogeneous `I`** (`GradedRing` instance) — the missing
143	  graded-quotient-**ring**; specialise to `I = (x)` for D3a. (Alt: D3b graded-subalgebra
144	  `GradedRing` + `RestrictScalars`.)
145	- **G4 — regrade `C`, `K` over `R/(x)`**: `SetLike.GradedSMul (R/(x))-grading (component
146	  family)`, reusing G1/G2 component families with the `R/(x)`-action.
147	- **G5 — `Module.Finite` transfer** via `Submodule.FG.restrictScalars_of_surjective`
148	  (`R ↠ R/(x)`), using annihilation by `(x)`.
149	- **Assembly**: degreewise SES + identity via rank–nullity (D5, no graded API);
150	  induction on `r` (number of degree-1 generators) with base `R = κ`; inductive step feeds
151	  `hC', hK'` (from IH on `C`, `K` over `R/(x)`, via G1–G5) and `hdiff` (D5) into the
152	  existing `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` + `IsRatHilb.bump`.
153	
154	G1 and G2 are the load-bearing, reusable, PR-quality core; G3 is the heaviest single
155	piece. Order the next-iter mathlib-build lane G1 → G2 → G5 → G3 → G4 → assembly, since
156	G1/G2/G5 unblock the most with the least risk, and the degreewise identity can be landed
157	independently at any time.

3	description: Mathlib isDefEq/whnf runaway over subtype/quotient module DirectSum.IsInternal — blocks the graded-module API G2–G4
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: f2d17d1e-5fc0-4d4f-9a16-2f07d339f733
8	---
9	
10	In `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean`'s graded-module API build (Stacks 00K1, namespace
11	`AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule`), any goal whose **type** mentions `DirectSum.IsInternal`,
12	`Submodule.map_iSup`, or an `iSup` over a family valued in `Submodule R ↥p` (subtype module) or
13	`Submodule R (M ⧸ p)` (quotient module) throws the elaborator into a runaway `isDefEq`/`whnf`
14	reduction of `DirectSum.coeLinearMap`/`coeAddMonoidHom` over the subtype/quotient. Minimal,
15	hypothesis-only repros loop at **200k and 2 000 000 heartbeats** — effectively non-terminating, not
16	slow. Discovered iter-015.
17	
18	**Why:** the natural Lean encoding of "induced grading on a derived module" (G2 `M/xM`, G3 `R/(x)`,
19	G4 regrade) is a decomposition of a quotient/subtype module — exactly the poisoned case.
20	
21	**How to apply:** State graded-module facts in the **ambient `M`** (independence + `⨆ = p`), never as
22	`IsInternal` on `↥p`/`M⧸p`. This is how G1 was salvaged into `homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep` +
23	`homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_inf_eq` (axiom-clean, ambient form).
24	
25	**RESOLVED iter-016 — Route 2 (ambient subquotient induction).** The mathlib-analogist verified that a
26	*conclusion-only* Hilbert-function restatement does NOT escape the blocker (applying the IH to `C`/`K`
27	still instantiates the carrier to `M⧸xM`/`↥K`). The sound fix re-ranges the WHOLE induction over
28	**pairs of ambient homogeneous submodules `N'≤N` of a fixed `M`** with `r` commuting degree-+1 endos,
29	concluding `IsRatHilb` of the ambient difference `n ↦ dim_κ(N⊓ℳn) − dim_κ(N'⊓ℳn)`. ker/coker of a
30	degree-1 endo stay ambient subquotient pairs (`K=(N⊓x⁻¹N',N')`, `C=(N,N'+x·N)`), so no derived-carrier
31	object is ever formed. Eliminates G2/G3/G4, reduces G5 to ambient FG bookkeeping, reuses G1 + D5 +
32	`IsRatHilb`. Full plan + ordered build list: `analogies/quot-hilbert-function-route.md`; blueprint
33	re-skeletoned in `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (`lem:graded_subquotient_*`). Fallback for a genuine future
34	derived-carrier graded object: `DirectSum.Decomposition.ofLinearMap` (avoids the `Bijective`/whnf
35	unfold). Related: [[quot-graded-module-api-build-order]].

10	016
11	
12	## Question
13	Two sub-questions, both about escaping the `coeLinearMap`/`coeAddMonoidHom` whnf
14	runaway that blocked G2–G4 in iter-015:
15	1. Is there a Mathlib-idiomatic *tame encoding* of a `DirectSum.Decomposition` on a
16	   quotient/subtype carrier that avoids the runaway?
17	2. Can the Stacks-00K1 induction be restated at the level of Hilbert *functions*
18	   (`n ↦ dim_κ Mₙ`) so the inductive step never builds a quotient-module
19	   `Decomposition` at all — and if so, what is the MINIMAL structure on `C = M/xM`
20	   and `K = ker x` that must actually be built?
21	
22	## The runaway, mechanically (verified this iter)
23	- `DirectSum.IsInternal A` is *by definition* `Function.Bijective (DirectSum.coeAddMonoidHom A)`
24	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Basic`; confirmed via hover/loogle). Any goal that unfolds
25	  `IsInternal` on a derived carrier (subtype `↥p`, quotient `M ⧸ p`) forces `isDefEq`/`whnf`
26	  to reduce `coeAddMonoidHom`/`coeLinearMap` over that carrier → non-terminating
27	  (looped at 200k and 2,000,000 heartbeats in the prover's repros).
28	- `DirectSum.Decomposition.isInternal` and `isInternal_submodule_of_iSupIndep_of_iSup_eq_top`
29	  (the path the iter-015 prover used) both route through this `Bijective`/`coeAddMonoidHom`
30	  unfold — hence the timeout.
31	- `Submodule.map_iSup` over a family valued in `Submodule R (M ⧸ p)` triggers the same
32	  reduction even at *statement* time.
33	- G1 was rescued precisely by **never leaving the ambient `M`**: it states
34	  `iSupIndep (fun i => ℳ i ⊓ p)` and `⨆ i, (ℳ i ⊓ p) = p` — families valued in
35	  `Submodule R M`. Ambient internal gradings and `finrank` of subtype/quotient pieces
36	  (`↥(N ⊓ ℳ n)`, `W ⧸ range φ` in D5) are SAFE; only derived-carrier
37	  `IsInternal`/`Decomposition`/`map_iSup` are toxic.
38	
39	## Decisions identified
40	
41	### Decision Q1: tame encoding of a derived-carrier `DirectSum.Decomposition`
42	- **Mathlib idiom**: `DirectSum.Decomposition.ofLinearMap (ℳ) (decompose) (h_left_inv) (h_right_inv)`
43	  — `Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Decomposition`. Signature:
44	  `decompose : M →ₗ[R] ⨁ i, ↥(ℳ i)`, `h_left_inv : coeLinearMap ℳ ∘ₗ decompose = id`,
45	  `h_right_inv : decompose ∘ₗ coeLinearMap ℳ = id`. It builds the instance WITHOUT ever
46	  unfolding to `Bijective`, so the `IsInternal`-whnf trigger never fires. The two
47	  identities can be discharged per-component with `DirectSum.coeLinearMap_of`
48	  (`coeLinearMap ℳ (of _ i x) = ↑x`) + `DirectSum.linearMap_ext`, i.e. a controlled
49	  `ext`-based proof, NOT a blind `isDefEq`. Precedent for the equiv-based handle:
50	  `DirectSum.decomposeLinearEquiv` and `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Graded` (graded Lie algebras
51	  manipulate the decomposition through `decomposeLinearEquiv`, never raw `IsInternal`).
52	- **Caveat**: `ofLinearMap` tames the *instance construction*, but you must still build the
53	  quotient `decompose` map and prove the two identities. If that proof reaches for
54	  `Submodule.map_iSup` on the quotient family, the runaway returns. So it is a viable
55	  escape for a SINGLE derived-carrier grading done carefully, not a free pass — and it
56	  does nothing for the missing graded quotient RING (G3): Mathlib has zero `⧸` in
57	  `RingTheory/GradedAlgebra` (`GradedRing` + `HasQuotient` loogle empty this iter).
58	- **Gap**: divergent-and-missing, but buildable. **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL
59	  (achievable; keep as documented FALLBACK).
60	
61	### Decision Q2: is the Hilbert-function pivot sound, and what is the minimal C/K structure?
62	- **The pivot as the prover framed it (quantify the CONCLUSION over `n ↦ dim_κ Mₙ`) is NOT
63	  enough.** Applying the IH to `K` instantiates the IH's *carrier* to `↥K` and to `C`
64	  instantiates it to `M ⧸ xM`; if the IH's hypothesis demands a `DirectSum.Decomposition`
65	  on that carrier, you are back to the blocked subtype/quotient construction. This is the
66	  iter-014 Q3 obstruction ("the recursion's payload IS the graded structure") and it
67	  survives a conclusion-only restatement. To escape, the IH's **hypothesis** must also be
68	  derived-carrier-free.
69	- **The sound restatement: range the induction over SUBQUOTIENTS of a single FIXED ambient
70	  graded module.** Fix `ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M` with `[DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]` (ambient
71	  — safe). Quantify over a pair of homogeneous submodules `N' ≤ N ⊆ M` plus `r` pairwise-
72	  commuting degree-+1 κ-endomorphisms `t : Fin r → (M →ₗ[κ] M)` preserving `N` and `N'`.
73	  Hilbert function defined AMBIENT-LY:
74	  `h(n) = (finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) − finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ n)`  (cast to ℚ for `IsRatHilb`).
75	  Conclusion: `IsRatHilb h r`.
76	  - This class is **closed** under the two operations of the SES, with both results
77	    annihilated by the killed endo `x = t_{r-1}`, and both expressed as ambient pairs:
78	    - `K = ker(x)` subquotient `= (N ⊓ x⁻¹(N'), N')`  (preimage is a homogeneous submodule of `M`);
79	    - `C = coker(x) = N/(N' + x·N)` subquotient `= (N, N' + x·N)`  (`N' + x·N` homogeneous in `M`).
80	    A subquotient of a subquotient of `M` is again a subquotient of `M` — the framework
81	    never produces a derived carrier.
82	  - **Minimal structure on `C` and `K`: NONE on a quotient/subtype carrier.** `C` is just
83	    the ambient pair `(⊤, x·M)` of homogeneous submodules and the ambient dimension-
84	    difference function `n ↦ dim(ℳ n) − dim(x·M ⊓ ℳ n)`; `x·M ⊓ ℳ n = x·(ℳ (n-1))`
85	    is an ambient identity (x homogeneous of degree 1 + G1). `K` is the pair `(ker x, ⊥)`.
86	    No `DirectSum.Decomposition (M ⧸ xM)`, no `DirectSum.Decomposition ↥K`, no
87	    `GradedRing (R ⧸ (x))` is ever formed.
88	- **Why subquotients and not just submodules**: `coker(x) = N/xN` is killed by `x` (so it is
89	  in the (r−1)-endo IH domain), but `xN ⊆ N` is NOT killed by `x`, so the
90	  "`h_C = h_N − h_{xN}` via `IsRatHilb.sub`" shortcut fails — `xN` is not an IH object. The
91	  minimal class closed under ker+coker of a degree-1 endo starting from `M` is exactly
92	  subquotients. (`IsRatHilb.sub` is still used internally, on `h_C` and shifted `h_K`, by
93	  the already-landed `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq`.)
94	- **Finiteness (the residual G5)**: carry "`M` f.g. over `κ[t_0,…,t_{r-1}]`" (equivalently
95	  `Module.Finite`); the base `r = 0` gives a finite-dimensional subquotient ⇒ `h` eventually
96	  `0` ⇒ `IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero`. Sub/quotient + Noetherian + annihilation-by-`x`
97	  transfers f.g. down a generator (`Submodule.FG`, `Module.Finite.quotient`,
98	  `Submodule.FG.restrictScalars_of_surjective`). This stays ambient (it is about FG of
99	  ambient submodules, never a derived-carrier grading).
100	- **Gap**: this is a project-design restatement that AVOIDS the Mathlib gap entirely.
101	  **Verdict**: PROCEED (build the ambient subquotient induction; it sidesteps G2–G4).
102	
103	## Recommendation
104	
105	Adopt **Route 2 (the ambient subquotient induction)** — it is the sound realization of the
106	prover's Hilbert-function pivot and the only route that makes the runaway *structurally
107	impossible*, because every object that ever appears is an ambient family
108	`fun n => Naux ⊓ ℳ n` of submodules of the FIXED `M`, exactly the shape G1 proved safe.
109	It eliminates G2, G3 and G4 as originally specified (no quotient-module Decomposition, no
110	graded quotient ring, no regrading) and reduces G5 to ambient FG bookkeeping. Keep
111	**Route 1 (`DirectSum.Decomposition.ofLinearMap`)** documented as the fallback for any
112	future lemma that genuinely needs an honest graded object on a derived carrier.
113	
114	### Residual build list (Route 2), ordered
115	
116	1. **`SubquotientHilb` data + ambient Hilbert function** (new). Package: fixed `ℳ` with
117	   `[DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]`, finite-dim components; homogeneous `N' ≤ N`; commuting
118	   degree-+1 endos `t : Fin r → M →ₗ[κ] M` preserving `N`, `N'`; `Module.Finite` witness.
119	   Define `hilb : ℕ → ℚ := fun n => ((finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) − finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ n))`.
120	   Reuses G1 (`homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_inf_eq`, `homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep`).
121	2. **Ambient `ker`/`coker` closure lemmas** (new): build the two subquotient pairs above,
122	   prove homogeneity of `N ⊓ x⁻¹(N')` and `N' + x·N` (G1 + `SetLike.IsHomogeneousElem.graded_smul`),
123	   prove the remaining endos preserve them and that `x` kills both.
124	3. **Degreewise difference D6** (new, small): identify the component dims of the `K`/`C`
125	   subquotients with `ker(x : ℳn→ℳ(n+1))` and `ℳ(n+1)/im(x)`, then apply the landed
126	   **D5** (`degreewise_finrank_diff`) to get `hilb_M(n+1) − hilb_M(n) = hilb_C(n+1) − hilb_K(n)`.
127	4. **Finiteness transfer (residual G5, ambient)** (new, low effort): f.g. of the two
128	   subquotients over `κ[t_0..t_{r-2}]` via Noetherian + `Submodule.FG` + annihilation by `x`.
129	5. **The induction `P(r)`** (new): base `r=0` via `IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero`; step via
130	   (2)+(3)+(4) feeding the landed `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` and `IsRatHilb.bump`.
131	6. **Bridge to `AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`** (new, thin):
132	   instantiate `P` at `(N,N') = (⊤,⊥)`, endos = multiplication by the degree-1 generators;
133	   derive the `r` degree-1 generators from "f.g. κ-algebra generated in degree 1"
134	   (blueprint NOTE, `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` L429–435).
135	
136	Landed and reused as-is: **G1** (`homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep`,
137	`homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_inf_eq`), **D5** (`degreewise_finrank_diff`), the entire
138	`IsRatHilb` toolkit (`ofEventuallyZero`, `bump`, `sub`, `shiftRight`, `antidiff`,
139	`ofDiffEq`). **Dropped from the iter-014 plan**: G2 (quotient Decomposition), G3 (graded
140	quotient ring), G4 (regrade over R/(x)) — none are built under Route 2.

/-! ### Ambient subquotient induction for Stacks 00K1

The Route-2 graded-module side of the inductive step. Everything is phrased over a
**fixed** ambient graded `κ`-module `M = ⨁ ℳ n`: a subquotient is a pair of homogeneous
submodules `N' ≤ N ⊆ M`, and its Hilbert function is the ambient dimension difference
`n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)`. The kernel and cokernel of a degree-one
endomorphism are again ambient subquotient pairs, so no `DirectSum.Decomposition` on a
quotient/subtype carrier is ever formed (cf.
`memory/graded-quotient-module-isdefeq-pathology.md`). -/

section Subquotient

variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
variable (ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]

/-- A `κ`-linear endomorphism `x` of `M` **raises degree by one** with respect to the
grading `ℳ` when `x (ℳ n) ⊆ ℳ (n+1)` for every `n`. This is the abstract, graded-ring-free
form of "multiplication by a degree-one element" used in the Stacks 00K1 induction.
Project-local. -/
def RaisesDegree (x : M →ₗ[κ] M) : Prop := ∀ n, (ℳ n).map x ≤ ℳ (n + 1)

/-- Membership form of `RaisesDegree`. -/
lemma RaisesDegree.mem {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x) {n : ℕ} {m : M}
    (hm : m ∈ ℳ n) : x m ∈ ℳ (n + 1) :=
  hx n (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm)

/-- The **ambient subquotient Hilbert function** of a pair of homogeneous submodules
`N' ≤ N ⊆ M`: `n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)` (computed in `ℤ`, cast to `ℚ`).
This is the data the Stacks 00K1 induction tracks; it depends only on the ambient
intersections `N ⊓ ℳ n`, `N' ⊓ ℳ n` of submodules of the fixed `M`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def subquotientHilb (N N' : Submodule κ M) (n : ℕ) : ℚ :=
  (((Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) - (Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) : ℤ) : ℚ)

end Subquotient

end GradedModule

noncomputable def subquotientHilb (N N' : Submodule κ M) (n : ℕ) : ℚ :=
  (((Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) - (Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) : ℤ) : ℚ)

/-- A degree-raising endomorphism shifts the homogeneous decomposition: the degree-`(i+1)`
component of `x m` is `x` applied to the degree-`i` component of `m`. This is the ambient
commutation fact that makes preimages and images of homogeneous submodules under `x`
homogeneous. Project-local. -/
lemma decompose_raisesDegree {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x) (m : M) (i : ℕ) :

/-- A degree-raising endomorphism kills the degree-zero component: `x m` has no degree-`0`
part. Project-local. -/
lemma decompose_raisesDegree_zero {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x) (m : M) :
    (DirectSum.decompose ℳ (x m) 0 : M) = 0 := by
  classical
  conv_lhs => rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose ℳ m, map_sum, DirectSum.decompose_sum]
  simp only [DirectSum.sum_apply, AddSubmonoidClass.coe_finset_sum]
  refine Finset.sum_eq_zero fun j _ => ?_
  rw [DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_ne ℳ
    (hx j (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (SetLike.coe_mem _))) (by omega : j + 1 ≠ 0)]

/-- The image of a homogeneous submodule under a degree-raising endomorphism is
homogeneous. Project-local. -/
lemma map_isHomogeneous {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
    {N : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) :
    (N.map x).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
  intro i z hz
  obtain ⟨m, hm, rfl⟩ := hz
  cases i with
  | zero => rw [decompose_raisesDegree_zero ℳ hx m]; exact Submodule.zero_mem _
  | succ i =>
      rw [decompose_raisesDegree ℳ hx m i]
      exact Submodule.mem_map_of_mem ((Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hN).mp hm i)

/-- **Ambient image identity.** For a homogeneous submodule `N` and a degree-raising
endomorphism `x`, the degree-`(n+1)` part of `x · N` is `x · (N ⊓ ℳ n)`. Project-local. -/
lemma map_inf_degree_eq {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
    {N : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (n : ℕ) :
    N.map x ⊓ ℳ (n + 1) = (N ⊓ ℳ n).map x := by
  apply le_antisymm
  · rintro y ⟨hy1, hy2⟩
    obtain ⟨m, hm, rfl⟩ := hy1
    refine Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨DirectSum.decompose ℳ m n, ?_, ?_⟩
    · exact Submodule.mem_inf.mpr
        ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hN).mp hm n, SetLike.coe_mem _⟩
    · rw [decompose_raisesDegree ℳ hx m n, DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_same ℳ hy2]
  · refine le_inf (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_left) ?_
    rw [Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap]
    exact fun m hm => hx.mem ℳ (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hm).2

/-- **Ambient distributive law.** Intersecting a sum of two homogeneous submodules with a
graded piece distributes: `(P + Q) ⊓ ℳ k = (P ⊓ ℳ k) + (Q ⊓ ℳ k)`. Project-local. -/
lemma sup_inf_degree_eq {P Q : Submodule κ M}
    (hP : P.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (hQ : Q.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (k : ℕ) :
    (P ⊔ Q) ⊓ ℳ k = (P ⊓ ℳ k) ⊔ (Q ⊓ ℳ k) := by
  apply le_antisymm
  · rintro z ⟨hzPQ, hzk⟩
    obtain ⟨p, hp, q, hq, rfl⟩ := Submodule.mem_sup.mp hzPQ
    have hpk : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ p k : M) ∈ P ⊓ ℳ k :=
      Submodule.mem_inf.mpr ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hP).mp hp k, SetLike.coe_mem _⟩
    have hqk : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ q k : M) ∈ Q ⊓ ℳ k :=
      Submodule.mem_inf.mpr ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hQ).mp hq k, SetLike.coe_mem _⟩
    have hsum : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ p k : M) + (DirectSum.decompose ℳ q k : M) = p + q := by
      rw [← AddSubmonoidClass.coe_add, ← DirectSum.add_apply, ← DirectSum.decompose_add,
        DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_same ℳ hzk]
    exact hsum ▸ Submodule.add_mem_sup hpk hqk
  · exact sup_le (le_inf (inf_le_left.trans le_sup_left) inf_le_right)
      (le_inf (inf_le_left.trans le_sup_right) inf_le_right)

end Subquotient

/-- The dimension of the preimage of `S` under the inclusion of a submodule `p` equals the
dimension of the ambient intersection `p ⊓ S`. Project-local helper for the degreewise
difference identity. -/
private lemma finrank_comap_subtype (p S : Submodule κ M) :
    Module.finrank κ ↥(Submodule.comap p.subtype S) = Module.finrank κ ↥(p ⊓ S) := by
  rw [← Submodule.map_comap_subtype p S]
  exact (Submodule.equivMapOfInjective p.subtype p.injective_subtype _).finrank_eq

/-- **D6 — subquotient degreewise difference.** For a degree-raising endomorphism `x` and
homogeneous submodules `N`, `N'`, the first difference of the ambient subquotient Hilbert
function of `(N, N')` equals the alternating sum of the Hilbert functions of the cokernel
subquotient `C = (N, N' ⊔ x·N)` and kernel subquotient `K = (N ⊓ x⁻¹N', N')`:
`hilb(n+1) − hilb(n) = hilb_C(n+1) − hilb_K(n)`. This is the `hdiff` hypothesis consumed by
`IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` in the Stacks 00K1 induction. Project-local. -/
lemma subquotient_degreewise_diff {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
    {N N' : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (n : ℕ) :
    subquotientHilb ℳ N N' (n + 1) - subquotientHilb ℳ N N' n
      = subquotientHilb ℳ N (N' ⊔ N.map x) (n + 1)
        - subquotientHilb ℳ (N ⊓ N'.comap x) N' n := by
  classical
  haveI : FiniteDimensional κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) := Submodule.finiteDimensional_of_le inf_le_right
  haveI : FiniteDimensional κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) := Submodule.finiteDimensional_of_le inf_le_right
  set T : Submodule κ M := (N ⊓ ℳ n).map x with hTdef
  have hT : N.map x ⊓ ℳ (n + 1) = T := map_inf_degree_eq ℳ hx hN n
  have hTle : T ≤ ℳ (n + 1) := hT ▸ inf_le_right
  haveI : FiniteDimensional κ ↥T := Submodule.finiteDimensional_of_le hTle
  -- the two linear maps into `M ⧸ N'`
  set φ : ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) →ₗ[κ] (M ⧸ N') := (N'.mkQ).comp (x.comp (N ⊓ ℳ n).subtype) with hφ
  set g : ↥T →ₗ[κ] (M ⧸ N') := (N'.mkQ).comp T.subtype with hg
  have hrange : LinearMap.range φ = LinearMap.range g := by
    rw [hφ, hg, LinearMap.range_comp, LinearMap.range_comp, LinearMap.range_comp,
      Submodule.range_subtype, Submodule.range_subtype, hTdef]
  have hkerφ : Module.finrank κ ↥(LinearMap.ker φ)
      = Module.finrank κ ↥((N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n) := by
    have hk : LinearMap.ker φ = Submodule.comap (N ⊓ ℳ n).subtype (N'.comap x) := by
      rw [hφ, LinearMap.ker_comp, Submodule.ker_mkQ, Submodule.comap_comp]
    rw [hk, finrank_comap_subtype]
    congr 1
    rw [inf_right_comm]
  have hkerg : Module.finrank κ ↥(LinearMap.ker g) = Module.finrank κ ↥(T ⊓ N') := by
    rw [hg, LinearMap.ker_comp, Submodule.ker_mkQ, finrank_comap_subtype]
  -- inclusion–exclusion linking the cokernel block to `b` and `T`
  have hC : (N' ⊔ N.map x) ⊓ ℳ (n + 1) = (N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) ⊔ T := by
    rw [sup_inf_degree_eq ℳ hN' (map_isHomogeneous ℳ hx hN), hT]
  have hinf : (N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) ⊓ T = T ⊓ N' := by
    rw [inf_right_comm, inf_of_le_left (le_trans inf_le_right hTle), inf_comm]
  have hIE := Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq (N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) T
  rw [← hC, hinf] at hIE
  -- the two rank–nullity identities
  have RN := LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker φ
  have RG := LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker g
  rw [hkerφ] at RN
  rw [hkerg, ← hrange] at RG
  -- the integer dimension identity
  have key : Module.finrank κ ↥((N' ⊔ N.map x) ⊓ ℳ (n + 1))
              + Module.finrank κ ↥((N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n)
           = Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n)
              + Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) := by
    omega
  -- assemble
  simp only [subquotientHilb]
  push_cast
  have keyQ : (Module.finrank κ ↥((N' ⊔ N.map x) ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) : ℚ)
              + Module.finrank κ ↥((N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n)
           = Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) + Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) := by
    exact_mod_cast key
  linarith [keyQ]

end Subquotient

input.new_string:
/-- The intersection of two homogeneous submodules is homogeneous. Project-local: Mathlib
provides no lattice-closure lemmas for `Submodule.IsHomogeneous`. -/
lemma inf_isHomogeneous {p q : Submodule κ M} (hp : p.IsHomogeneous ℳ)
    (hq : q.IsHomogeneous ℳ) : (p ⊓ q).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
  intro i z hz
  exact Submodule.mem_inf.mpr
    ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hp).mp (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hz).1 i,
      (Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hq).mp (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hz).2 i⟩

/-- The sum (supremum) of two homogeneous submodules is homogeneous. Project-local. -/
lemma sup_isHomogeneous {p q : Submodule κ M} (hp : p.IsHomogeneous ℳ)
    (hq : q.IsHomogeneous ℳ) : (p ⊔ q).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
  intro i z hz
  obtain ⟨a, ha, b, hb, rfl⟩ := Submodule.mem_sup.mp hz
  have hcoe : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ (a + b) i : M)
      = ↑(DirectSum.decompose ℳ a i) + ↑(DirectSum.decompose ℳ b i) := by
    rw [DirectSum.decompose_add, DirectSum.add_apply]; rfl
  rw [hcoe]
  exact Submodule.add_mem_sup ((Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hp).mp ha i)
    ((Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hq).mp hb i)

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean — iter-017 (QUOT Route-2, first dispatch)

## Summary

- **Declarations added: 13, all axiom-clean** (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`),
  no `sorry`, no new `maxHeartbeats`. All in `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GradedModule`,
  `section Subquotient` (appended after the landed D5 `degreewise_finrank_diff`).
- **Sorry count in file: 4 → 4** (unchanged — only the 4 pre-existing protected stubs at
  lines 123/161/198/225 use `sorry`; none of my declarations do).
- **Keystone landed:** `subquotient_degreewise_diff` (D6, `lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff`)
  — the mathematically hardest *ambient* lemma of the Route-2 induction, the `hdiff` input to
  `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq`.
- **Blocked on: `subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational` (the `P(r)` induction)** — the genuine
  block is the *finiteness encoding* (`Module.Finite (κ[t₀..t_{r-1}]) M` from `r` commuting
  degree-1 endomorphisms). Scouted the exact tool path (`Algebra.adjoinCommRingOfComm`); it is
  a large self-contained build, deferred to a dedicated next-iter lane to avoid a sorried state.
- **HARD ENTRY CONSTRAINT honored:** no `DirectSum.Decomposition`/`IsInternal`/`map_iSup` on any
  quotient/subtype carrier was ever written. Every object is an ambient family
  `fun n => Naux ⊓ ℳ n` of submodules of the fixed `M`. **No isDefEq/whnf recurrence was
  encountered** at any point (the memory-flagged pathology did not fire).

## Declarations added (all axiom-clean)

Blueprint-pinned (names match existing `\lean{}` pins exactly):
1. `subquotientHilb` (def, ~line 712) — `def:graded_subquotientHilb`. The ambient subquotient
   Hilbert function `n ↦ ((finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) − finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ n) : ℚ)`.
   *Note:* realized as a function of the pair `(N, N')` only; the endo-family + `Module.Finite`
   "datum" of the blueprint definition is carried as **hypotheses of the induction lemma**, not
   bundled into `subquotientHilb` (the Hilbert function genuinely depends only on `(N, N')`).
2. `subquotient_degreewise_diff` (D6, ~line 855) — `lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff`.
   `hilb(n+1) − hilb(n) = hilb_C(n+1) − hilb_K(n)` with `C = (N, N' ⊔ N.map x)`,
   `K = (N ⊓ N'.comap x, N')`. **DONE.**

New auxiliary infrastructure (`lean_aux` — need blueprint entries, see below):
3. `RaisesDegree` (def) — predicate `∀ n, (ℳ n).map x ≤ ℳ (n+1)` (graded-ring-free "mul by
   degree-1 element"). `RaisesDegree.mem` (membership form).
4. `decompose_raisesDegree` — `↑(decompose ℳ (x m) (i+1)) = x ↑(decompose ℳ m i)` (degree-shift
   commutation; the load-bearing decomposition fact). `decompose_raisesDegree_zero` — degree-0
   part of `x m` is 0.
5. `comap_isHomogeneous`, `map_isHomogeneous` — preimage / image of a homogeneous submodule
   under a degree-raising endo is homogeneous.
6. `inf_isHomogeneous`, `sup_isHomogeneous` — lattice closure of `Submodule.IsHomogeneous`
   (Mathlib has none). Together with (5) these give the full **homogeneity content of
   `lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker`**: `N ⊓ N'.comap x` and `N' ⊔ N.map x` are homogeneous.
7. `map_inf_degree_eq` — ambient image identity `N.map x ⊓ ℳ(n+1) = (N ⊓ ℳ n).map x`.
8. `sup_inf_degree_eq` — ambient distributive law `(P ⊔ Q) ⊓ ℳ k = (P⊓ℳk) ⊔ (Q⊓ℳk)` for
   homogeneous `P, Q`.
9. `finrank_comap_subtype` (private) — `finrank ↥(comap p.subtype S) = finrank ↥(p ⊓ S)`.

Section hypotheses: `{κ M} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]`,
`(ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]`; D6 additionally assumes
`[∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ ↥(ℳ n)]` (finite-dimensional graded components).

## subquotient_degreewise_diff (D6) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean

- **Approach:** single κ-linear map `φ : ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) →ₗ[κ] M ⧸ N'`, `φ = N'.mkQ ∘ x ∘ subtype`,
  plus the companion `g : ↥T →ₗ[κ] M ⧸ N'` with `T := (N ⊓ ℳ n).map x`. Two rank–nullity
  identities (`LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`), `ker φ`/`ker g` computed via
  `finrank_comap_subtype`, `range φ = range g`, plus inclusion–exclusion
  (`Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq`) rewritten by the ambient image identity (7) and
  distributive law (8). Reduces to the ℕ identity `c_{n+1} + k_n = a_n + b_{n+1}`, closed by
  `omega`; final ℚ assembly by `simp only [subquotientHilb]; push_cast; linarith`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. Does **not** require `N' ≤ N` (the single-map route avoids it; the
  blueprint datum has `N'≤N`, harmless to keep as a hypothesis later).
- **Dead end avoided:** the blueprint's "induced map on subquotients `Q_n → Q_{n+1}` + D5" route
